title: H-Null Modularity PASS: Les Miserables hub-dominated network
_fixture_purpose: Certifies H-null modularity model on Les Miserables co-appearance network (77 nodes, 254 edges). H-null ARI=0.638 vs standard ARI=0.588 (+0.050). Hub-dominated topology where community-internal hubs benefit from asymmetric null. HONEST: wins on 1/10 benchmark graphs only.

h_null_model.definition: H(b,e) = C + F where C = 2*d*e (green quadrance), F = d*d - e*e (red quadrance)
h_null_model.degree_mapping: b = max(k_i, k_j), e = min(k_i, k_j), d = b + e = k_i + k_j
h_null_model.h_vs_x_ratio: H/X = b/e + 4 + 2*e/b grows linearly with degree asymmetry r=b/e
h_null_model.chromogeometry: H = C + F = green + red quadrance (Wildberger chromogeometry)

mathematical_identity.F: d*d - e*e = (b+e)*(b+e) - e*e = b*b + 2*b*e = b*(b+2*e) = b*a = red quadrance Qr(d,e)

primary_result.direction: H-null outperforms standard on hub-dominated networks

secondary_results.note: All QA null models tie — low degree variance means null model choice irrelevant

honest_negatives.interpretation: H-null is topology-specific, NOT a universal improvement. Effect requires hub-dominated networks with community-internal hubs where degree asymmetry between node pairs carries structural information.

axiom_compliance.A2: d=b+e, a=b+2*e derived correctly (never assigned independently)
axiom_compliance.T2: ARI/NMI scores and community assignments are observer projections only
axiom_compliance.S1: no **2 in QA computation — d*d not d**2
axiom_compliance.S2: all QA state is integer (degrees, C, F, G, H values)

witnesses.observation: H-null captures both symmetric (C=2de) and asymmetric (F=b*a) degree relationships; standard null X=k_i*k_j misses the additive component through d=k_i+k_j
